mission.title: Install and Configure NMstate
mission.description: NMstate is a CNCF declarative network management API for Linux hosts. It provides a Python library (libnmstate), Rust crate, and CLI tools (nmstatectl) for managing network configurations declaratively.

steps.title: Install NMstate via package manager
steps.description:
Install NMstate using your system package manager.

```bash
# Fedora/RHEL
sudo dnf install nmstate

# Or via pip
pip install libnmstate
```

steps.title: Verify installation
steps.description:
Check the NMstate CLI is available.

```bash
nmstatectl version
```

steps.title: Show current network state
steps.description:
Display the current declarative network state.

```bash
nmstatectl show
```

> This outputs the full network configuration in YAML format.

uninstall.title: Uninstall NMstate
uninstall.description:
Remove NMstate.

```bash
sudo dnf remove nmstate
# Or: pip uninstall libnmstate -y
```
